name: Census Reverse ETL API
what_it_does: Reverse ETL platform with REST API for syncing data from data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) to operational tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, Braze) — enabling activation of warehouse data without custom ETL pipelines or manual exports.
use_cases: Syncing customer segments from Snowflake to Salesforce for sales team outreach
Triggering personalized email campaigns in Braze based on warehouse-computed segments
Keeping HubSpot contacts in sync with warehouse-derived product usage data
Automating reverse ETL pipeline runs via API on warehouse model completion
Monitoring sync health and failure notifications via Census API
not_for: Forward ETL (moving data INTO warehouse — use Fivetran or Airbyte)
Real-time streaming data sync (Census is batch-oriented warehouse sync)
best_when: A data team has a warehouse with computed models and needs to sync those insights to operational tools (CRM, marketing, support) without building custom pipelines.
avoid_when: You don't have a data warehouse, need real-time sync, or need to move data into (not out of) a warehouse.

auth.notes: API bearer token from workspace settings. Workspace-scoped access. OAuth2 for service connections (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.). No fine-grained API scopes. Separate credentials per destination.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 14-day free trial; no permanent free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Platform from $500/month; Enterprise custom based on destinations and volume
pricing.notes: No free tier — enterprise-oriented pricing. US-based startup with strong VC backing (Andreessen Horowitz). Strong Snowflake and dbt integration. Pricing based on connected destinations and rows synced. Competitive with Hightouch.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per plan; sync frequency configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for managing syncs, triggering runs, monitoring status. Webhook support for sync completion events. Good documentation with clear API reference. Sync runs are async — must poll for completion or use webhooks.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sync trigger creates new run — not idempotent. Sync configuration updates idempotent. Run status queries idempotent. Destination record upserts handled by Census connector logic.
known_agent_gotchas: Sync runs are async — must poll status or configure webhook for completion notification
Warehouse query execution adds latency — large models take minutes not seconds
Destination API rate limits (Salesforce, HubSpot) affect sync speed — not Census's limit
Full vs incremental sync distinction is critical — full sync can overwrite destination data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Warehouse credentials encrypted at rest. OAuth2 for destination connections. Data flows warehouse → destination — Census does not store row data long-term. EU data residency available.
